title: Venue & Travel
text: This page brings together practical information to help you plan your visit to Glasgow for the EnviBayes-INLA workshop.

title: Venue & Travel
text:
🏫 Venue Information
The workshop takes place across several buildings on the University of Glasgow’s Gilmorehill Campus. To help you find your way around, the map below shows where the workshop’s activities will be held, from tutorials and talks to catering and poster sessions. Building-specific details, including accessibility information, are provided below.










  
    
      
        Accessibility & Emergency — School of Maths & Stats
        
      
      
        Accessibility
        A passenger lift serves every level.
        The main entrance has electric doors; they lock at 17:00, so please re-enter with a valid University ID card.
        In case of emergency
        Each staircase has a refuge area at the top for anyone who needs to wait for assistance.
        The assembly point for this building is outside the British Heart Foundation Building on University Place.
      
    
  



  
    
      
        Accessibility & Emergency — Boyd Orr Building
        
      
      
        Accessibility
        A fully step-free entrance is available from Lilybank Gardens.
        In case of emergency
        
          Proceed calmly to the assembly point at the corner of University Avenue and Ashton Road.
          An automated external defibrillator is mounted just outside the janitors' office on the ground floor.
          University of Glasgow staff and students should open the SafeZone phone application to alert Security if assistance is required.
        
      
    
  



  
    
      
        Accessibility & Emergency — Atrium, Wolfson Building
        
      
      
        The Atrium is on the ground floor and is completely step-free.
        If the fire alarm sounds, leave by the nearest exit and gather at the bench area directly outside the main entrance.
      
    
  



  
    
      
        Accessibility & Emergency — Glasgow University Union
        
      
      
        Please visit the GUU website for full accessibility information.

title: Venue & Travel
text:
🏨 Accommodation
The University of Glasgow is situated in the West End, with a variety of small hotels and guest houses nearby, as well as easy links to City Centre hotels via the Subway.
The closest Subway station to the main University campus is Hillhead. For City Centre hotels, the closest stations are Buchanan Street and St. Enoch. The journey between the West End and City Centre takes around 10 minutes by Subway.
Delegates are advised to book early to guarantee availability at their preferred hotel.
 Download Accomodation details PDF

title: Venue & Travel
text:
✈️ Getting to Glasgow
Guidance on arriving by air (Glasgow and Edinburgh airports), rail connections to Glasgow Central and Queen Street stations, and intercity coach services — via VisitGlasgow’s official travel guide.
View full travel guide →

🛂 Visa Information
Delegates travelling from outside the UK should check whether they need a visa or an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) to attend. As of February 2026, most visa-exempt visitors (including from the EU, US, Canada, and Australia) now require an ETA before travelling — this is separate from a visa and is usually approved within a few days, but we recommend applying at least 2–3 weeks before travel. Check requirements via the UK government’s visa/ETA checker.






NoteInvitation letter for visa application



If you require an official letter of invitation to support your visa or ETA application, please tick the related box in the registration form. If you forget to do this or require further assistance, please get in touch with the organising committee with your name, affiliation, and passport details as they appear on your travel documents.

title: Venue & Travel
text:
🚇 Exploring & Getting Around Glasgow
Glasgow is a vibrant and compact city with plenty to offer delegates outside of conference sessions.
The city has a range of cultural attractions including the works of Charles Rennie Mackintosh and many museums and art galleries offering free entry.
If you’re extending your stay, Glasgow has excellent travel connections for exploring Scotland and an exciting selection of year-round tours leaving daily for the Scottish Highlands.
Delegates can look forward to a warm welcome in a city voted ‘world’s friendliest city’ by Rough Guides.
Plan your trip at: visitglasgow.com
Getting around guide →
Things to do →

title: Workshops
text:
The following workshops will be delivered prior to the EnviBayes-INLA workshop.






NoteINLA AND BEYOND: Bayesian Hierarchical Modelling with inlabru




Jafet Belmont Osuna, University of Glasgow

INLA, a powerful tool for efficiently fitting complex spatial models, comes with a steep learning curve. This workshop introduces inlabru – a user-friendly interface to INLA. Learn to specify models intuitively, handle spatial data efficiently, and focus on data structures and interpretation relevant to ecology, rather than computational complexity!
Duration: 3 hours
Date: Monday 21\(^\text{st}\) June, 2027
Time: TBC
Venue: TBC

title: EnviBayes-INLA workshop
text: The EnviBayes–INLA Workshop 2027 is the first joint meeting between the EnviBayes and INLA workshop series, bringing together researchers developing Bayesian methods for environmental, ecological, spatial and spatio-temporal statistics. The programme will feature keynote lectures, contributed talks, poster sessions, and hands-on tutorials, covering a broad range of topics including INLA, spatial modelling, extreme value analysis, climate and weather, ecology, epidemiology, and environmental applications. The workshop builds on the success of previous EnviBayes workshops (2023, 2025) and the INLA: Past, Present and Future workshop, creating a unique opportunity for collaboration between these two vibrant research communities.
